supply_chain.dependencies: Ground leases for land under towers with average remaining life of approximately 35 years; approximately 54% of towers are leased or subleased from AT&T and T-Mobile; fiber assets primarily located on public rights-of-way requiring franchise agreements and pole attachments
supply_chain.risks: Loss of ground lease rights could adversely affect business; dependence on rights-of-way and franchise agreements for fiber operations; reliance on CLEC status for access to rights-of-way; potential inability to renew ground leases or fiber access agreements on favorable terms

regulatory_risks: FCC and FAA regulations controlling siting, construction, modification, lighting and marking of towers
State and local zoning authorities' jurisdiction over tower and small cell siting
Environmental regulations under National Environmental Policy Act (NEPA) requiring environmental impact review
Occupational Safety and Health Administration guidelines regarding radio frequency exposure
Risk of changes to REIT qualification requirements under Internal Revenue Code
Potential loss of REIT status resulting in inability to deduct dividends and increased tax obligations
State authorizations including CLEC status required for telecommunications services
Compliance with federal, state, local and foreign environmental laws relating to hazardous substances
Potential changes to laws and regulations affecting communications infrastructure deployment
SEC climate-related disclosure rules and California carbon and climate disclosure laws
